title: The Champion Who Shocked Formula 1 | Emerson Fittipaldi | F1 Beyond The Grid Legends
description:
Why would a double F1 World Champion at the height of his powers walk away from a winning car? For the 1976 season, Emerson Fittipaldi stunned the sport by leaving McLaren and setting up his own team. He wanted a challenge, and he certainly got one.

50 years later, Emerson tells Tom Clarkson why he and his brother Wilson launched Copersucar, a team based in their home country of Brazil. He also remembers the dramatic events that led him to start his own team – winning the World Championship wit

He’s living his childhood dream and he’s loving it.

title: Colton Herta: Doing The Work | F1 Beyond The Grid Podcast
description:
Formula 1 has always been a dream for Colton Herta. Walking away from IndyCar to race in Formula 2 is a gamble. His reward, should he prove himself, could be an F1 drive with Cadillac. Colton has come close to F1 before. This time he’s determined to make it.

Speaking on the day he had a seat-fit for Cadillac’s F1 car, Colton Herta tells Tom Clarkson about growing up at racetracks with his father, IndyCar legend Bryan Herta, his love of motorsport and F1 deals that never happened with Sauber and

title: F1’s Biggest SCANDAL 😳 Crashgate Explained! #F1 #SingaporeGP #Controversy #formula1 #f1shorts
description:
🏁 Welcome to Curious F1 Girl — where we decode Formula 1 like never before.

From iconic rivalries to last-lap drama, every story in Formula One has layers… and we break them down.

The 2008 Singapore Grand Prix witnessed one of the biggest scandals in Formula One history.

Known as “Crashgate,” this controversy involved a deliberate crash that completely changed the outcome of the race.

A safety car at the perfect moment… a strategy turned upside down… and a championship ultimately decided by

title: The F1 Safety Car Driver! Bernd Maylander: 500 Races In The Safety Car | F1 Beyond The Grid
description:
At this year’s season-opening Australian Grand Prix, Bernd Maylander reached a remarkable milestone of 500 races as the FIA Safety Car Driver.

Speaking to Tom Clarkson, Bernd reflects on how the Safety Car and his job have evolved since his first race back in 2000 and some of the most iconic races he’s been involved in over the last 25 years.

He talks through what a Grand Prix weekend looks like for him, the role his co-driver Richard Darker plays and some of the challenges they face – like pi

title: Lambiase Leaving: What Next For Max + Red Bull? | F1 Nation Podcast
description:
Tom Clarkson is joined by former F1 driver Jolyon Palmer and IndyCar race winner James Hinchcliffe to discuss the news that Max Verstappen’s race engineer, GianPiero Lambiase, will leave Red Bull Racing in 2028 and join McLaren as their Chief Racing Officer.

What does Lambiase’s departure mean for Red Bull? How will it affect Verstappen’s future at the team? And what will GP bring to McLaren?

Plus, the guys are joined by McLaren’s Technical Director of Performance, Mark Temple, who says Merced

title: So You Think You Know F1 Drivers?
description:
Tune in as we put the F1 grid on the spot with questions we've never asked them before in 'So You Think You Know F1 Drivers?'.

00:00 - Intro
00:38 - What’s one thing people get wrong about you?
03:01 - What keeps you going on your worst days?

description:
From the first time she sat in a kart, aged 9, Doriane Pin has wanted to race in Formula 1. F1 Academy champion, Mercedes Development Driver, poised to drive an F1 car for the first time - Doriane is getting closer and closer to that dream.

Doriane tells Tom Clarkson her racing story so far, from feeling like a 'superhero' in her kart, to seeking sponsors at the age of 11, taking on the Le Mans 24 hours, and being spotted by the Mercedes Junior Programme.

title: 2026 F1 Japanese GP RACE analysis by Peter Windsor
description: Kimi Antonelli didn't make a great start from the Suzuka pole but he recovered well before taking full advantage of a timely Safety Car on lap 22: back in front at the restart, with Oscar Piastri's McLaren-Mercedes now in his mirrors, the young Italian then disappeared into the Japanese sunset, setting fastest lap as he secured his second win in a row. Oscar duly finished a gritty P2, beating Charles Leclerc's Ferrari to the line by 1.5sec, while George Russell could finish only P4 for Mercedes

description:
2026 F1 Japanese GP FRIDAY Practice  Analysis. 

Oscar Piastri tops Friday practice at Suzuka, leading both Mercedes drivers in a session that’s already exposing the circuit’s usual high-speed challenges. McLaren look especially strong through the Esses and into Degner, with Piastri’s balance allowing him to carry consistent minimum speed through the long-direction changes.

title: Juan Pablo Montoya's record-breaking lap of Monza | 2004 Italian GP
description:
Colombia's Juan Pablo Montoya averaged an eye-watering 259.83 km/h when setting his pole position lap around Monza back in 2004.

This stood as a record until 2018, when it was narrowly eclipsed by Kimi Raikkonen with an average speed of 263.587km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q5_fo16E4LU

Lewis Hamilton then lowered the record further in 2020, with a lap that averaged 264.363 km/h: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2f1PtJV0vIs

Five years later, Max Verstappen went even faster, with an avera
